questions.question: What is the initial design step when creating a new Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC)?
In digital IC design, what is the primary purpose of integrating "scan chains" into a circuit?
options: To improve circuit performance by reducing propagation delays.
To lower static power consumption by enabling power gating.
To enable efficient testing of sequential logic elements (flip-flops) for manufacturing defects.
To simplify the routing of high-speed clock signals.
To facilitate the concurrent execution of multiple instructions.

questions.question: In the MESI cache coherence protocol, what state does a cache block enter if a processor owns the most up-to-date copy of data, and that data has been modified by this processor but not yet written back to main memory, and no other processor has a copy?
Which of the following best describes the core principle of "formal verification" in digital IC design?
options: Simulating the design with a large number of random test vectors to achieve high coverage.
Comparing the gate-level netlist against the RTL for functional equivalence using simulation.
Mathematically proving the correctness of a design against a specification across all possible input sequences.
Measuring power consumption and performance metrics under various operating conditions.
Generating physical layout directly from RTL code for rapid prototyping.

questions.question: What is the primary material used as the semiconductor substrate in the vast majority of Integrated Circuits (ICs)?
